---
title: "Interventions: Vaccination Pulse"
author: "Simon Frost"
date: today
subtitle: "NetworkOutbreaks.jl Vignette 03"
engine: julia
julia:
  project: ".."
---

## Introduction

NetworkOutbreaks.jl supports **scheduled interventions** that modify
the simulation mid-run. This vignette demonstrates a vaccination pulse
using `ScheduledStateChange`: at a specified time, a fraction of the
population is moved from susceptible (S) to vaccinated (V).

## Setup

```{julia}
using NetworkOutbreaks
using Graphs
using StableRNGs
using Statistics
using Plots
```

## Model with vaccination compartment

We define an SIRV model: S → I → R (standard SIR) plus a V
compartment that vaccination moves susceptibles into.

```{julia}
sirv = OutbreakModel(
    [:S, :I, :R, :V],
    [false, true, false, false],
    [OutbreakTransition(:S, :I, 1/6, :infection; via = [:I]),
     OutbreakTransition(:I, :R, 0.25, :spontaneous)];
    name = :SIRV)
```

## Baseline: no intervention

```{julia}
N = 1000
g = erdos_renyi(N, 5 / (N - 1); rng = StableRNG(42))

spec = OutbreakSpec(model = sirv, network = g,
    initial = SeedFraction(:I => 0.01),
    tspan = (0.0, 40.0))

ens_base = simulate_ensemble(spec; nsims = 200, seed = 1, parallel = true)
t_base, μ_base = mean_curve(ens_base, :I)
```

## Vaccination pulse at t = 5

We vaccinate 30% of the population at t = 5:

```{julia}
plan = InterventionPlan([
    ScheduledStateChange(5.0, :V, 0.30),
])

ens_vax = simulate_ensemble(spec; nsims = 200, seed = 1,
    parallel = false, interventions = plan)
t_vax, μ_vax = mean_curve(ens_vax, :I)
```

```{julia}
#| label: fig-vaccination-pulse
#| fig-cap: "SIR epidemic on ER(1000, 5) with and without a 30% vaccination pulse at t=5."
plot(t_base, μ_base ./ N, label = "No intervention", lw = 2, color = :red,
     xlabel = "Time", ylabel = "Infected fraction",
     title = "Vaccination pulse at t=5 (30% coverage)")
plot!(t_vax, μ_vax ./ N, label = "30% vaccinated at t=5", lw = 2, color = :blue)
```

## Varying vaccination timing

```{julia}
#| label: fig-vaccination-timing
#| fig-cap: "Earlier vaccination is more effective at reducing the epidemic peak."
p = plot(t_base, μ_base ./ N, label = "No vax", lw = 2, color = :grey,
         xlabel = "Time", ylabel = "Infected fraction",
         title = "Vaccination timing (30% coverage)")
for (t_v, col) in [(2.0, :green), (5.0, :blue), (10.0, :orange), (15.0, :red)]
    plan_t = InterventionPlan([ScheduledStateChange(t_v, :V, 0.30)])
    ens_t = simulate_ensemble(spec; nsims = 200, seed = 1,
        parallel = false, interventions = plan_t)
    _, μ_t = mean_curve(ens_t, :I)
    plot!(p, t_base[1:length(μ_t)], μ_t ./ N,
          label = "Vax at t=$(Int(t_v))", lw = 2, color = col)
end
p
```

Earlier vaccination is far more effective.
